fixes.origin: query = "SELECT metric_name FROM metrics WHERE namespace = '" + namespace + "'"
fixes.replacement:
query = "SELECT metric_name FROM metrics WHERE namespace = ?"
cur.execute(query, (namespace,))

fixes.origin:
script = (
    "INSERT INTO audit_log(actor, message) VALUES ('"
    + actor
    + "', '"
    + message
    + "')"
)

cur.executescript(script)
fixes.replacement:
cur.execute(
    "INSERT INTO audit_log(actor, message) VALUES (?, ?)",
    (actor, message),
)

fixes.origin:
command = f"{binary} --scan {target}"

proc = subprocess.run(
    command,
    shell=True,
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
)
fixes.replacement:
binary = {"net-scan": "net-scan", "deep-scan": "deep-scan"}.get(binary, "net-scan")

proc = subprocess.run(
    [binary, "--scan", target],
    capture_output=True,
    text=True,
)

static_findings.message: Possible SQL injection: SQL query variable "query" appears to be built unsafely before execution.
static_findings.evidence: query = "SELECT metric_name FROM metrics WHERE namespace = '" + namespace + "'"

static_findings.message: subprocess execution may allow OS command injection if input is not controlled.
